overview: Selesnya Company is a Pioneer aggro archetype that aims to flood the board with low-cost creatures to apply early pressure and win games before opponents can stabilize. It leverages powerful enter-the-battlefield effects, static abilities, and card advantage engines like Enduring Innocence and Collected Company to control the board and outpace value decks. The deck is highly adaptable, utilizing flexible slots and a targeted sideboard to navigate a diverse metagame.
win_conditions: Flood the board with low-cost creatures to apply early pressure
Trigger Enduring Innocence repeatedly for card advantage and board presence
Use Collected Company to go wide and overwhelm opponents
Win before opponents can deploy sweepers or stabilize with value engines

keep_criteria: Hands with one-drop mana dorks like Llanowar Elves or Elvish Mystic
Hands containing efficient two-drops like Thalia or Kellan
Hands with a mix of lands and creatures to jump from one to three mana by turn two
mulligan_criteria: Hands heavy on lands or high-cost cards without early threats
Hands without a clear path to apply turn one or two pressure

examples.reason: Provides unconditional mana dorks and an efficient two-drop to jump from one to three mana by turn two.

early_game: Play unconditional mana dorks like Llanowar Elves and Elvish Mystic to accelerate mana
Cast efficient two-drops like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben to tax opponents and protect creatures
Aim to jump from one mana to three by turn two
mid_game: Deploy board interaction like Skyclave Apparition and Werefox Bodyguard
Use Anointed Peacekeeper to identify targets for Aven Interrupter
Cast Collected Company to flood the board and apply pressure
late_game: Utilize Shefet Dunes to increase pressure in longer games or mirrors
Maintain board presence with Enduring Innocence to outvalue opponents
Sideboard in attrition tools like Gideon, Ally of Zendikar or Elite Spellbinder against control

key_cards.name: Llanowar Elves
key_cards.notes: Unconditional one-drop dork to jump from one to three mana by turn two.

key_cards.name: Thalia, Guardian of Thraben
key_cards.notes: Ensures creatures survive sweepers, forces less explosive plays, and headaches non-creature spell decks.

key_cards.name: Aven Interrupter
key_cards.notes: Exiles key opponent spells, granting an extra turn of tempo; best saved for game-changing cards.

key_cards.name: Anointed Peacekeeper
key_cards.notes: Reveals top card to guide Aven Interrupter usage and deals with Planeswalkers or activated abilities.

key_cards.name: Archon of Emeria
key_cards.notes: Answers Rakdos Prowess and Izzet Phoenix while delaying nonbasic lands against combo decks.

key_cards.name: Enduring Innocence
key_cards.notes: Triggers with almost any creature for constant card advantage and returns as an enchantment if destroyed.

key_cards.name: Collected Company
key_cards.notes: Main way to go wide and play fair against value archetypes.

sequencing_tips: Play mana dorks before two-drops to ensure smooth mana curve and early pressure
Hold Aven Interrupter for key opponent spells rather than casting it immediately
Use Anointed Peacekeeper's reveal to decide whether to cast Aven Interrupter or play another creature
Prioritize untapped lands like Eiganjo and Boseiju to maintain aggressive tempo

matchups.advice: Sideboard in Brutal Cathar, Werefox Bodyguard, and Portable Hole. Use Thalia's tax to slow their explosive starts and target key creatures.
Sideboard in Voice of Resurgence and attrition tools. Apply early pressure before they stabilize with counters and sweepers.
Sideboard in Unlicensed Hearse or Rest in Peace. Use Archon of Emeria to delay nonbasic lands and disrupt their graveyard recursion.
Sideboard in Gideon, Ally of Zendikar for its Glorious Anthem ability. Use Shefet Dunes to increase pressure in the mirror.
Sideboard in Portable Hole and Unlicensed Hearse. Target Witch's Oven and disrupt their sacrifice outlets.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Selesnya Company is a very adaptable deck with a wide range of answers for different Metagames... one of the main ways for “go wide” Aggro decks to play fair games against value archetypes.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: one of the main ways for “go wide” Aggro decks to play fair games against value archetypes
By triggering with almost any creature we have, it gives us a constant source of card advantage while also being a threat on the board
prevent them from burying us in card advantage... win before a sweeper

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: Capitalizing on its ETB is essential to pressure the opponent, and it is best to save it for key cards that can turn the tide of the game.
Anointed Peacekeeper usually provides us the information that defines the best cast for Aven Interrupter.
we aim to use as many lands as possible that come into play untapped, while providing us with access to the mana colors we need at the right time.

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: specific answers against cards like Liliana of the Veil with Loxodon Smiter
deals with unwanted Planeswalkers and activated effects of other troublesome permanents, such as Agatha's Soul Cauldron
counter to Witch’s Oven in Jund Sacrifice
answer against two of the best decks in the format: Rakdos Prowess and Izzet Phoenix
